channel.title: Sqwod Daily
channel.description: Your 5-minute audio rundown of the business of fitness and wellness — every weekday. From Sqwod.
channel.itunes:author: Sqwod
channel.itunes:summary: Your 5-minute audio rundown of the business of fitness and wellness — every weekday. From Sqwod.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 23 Jul 2026
item.description: Garmin swallows TrainingPeaks, a $200 Whoop rival & recovery becomes the whole pitch • $200 Screenless: Garmin Takes a Shot at Whoop's Wrist — Garmin just dropped a $200 screen-free fitness band aimed squarely at Whoop's territory. For coaches and studio operators who recommend wearables to clients, there's a new name in the no-display game worth knowing. • DSSV drops 2026 DACH fitness industry benchmarks — The DSSV has unveiled the key figures shaping the German-speaking fitness market in 2026. If you run a gym or coach clients in the region, this is the data that tells you where the money and membership trends are actually heading. • body LIFE is gating a live studio-ops webinar behind a password — The protected session promises top-tier expertise delivered directly to studio owners and coaches — if you're in the industry, hunting down that access code is worth five minutes of your morning. • Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts — Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for movement as a therapeutic tool — a timely reminder that what you're selling in your studio isn't just fitness, it's function and wellbeing. • Garmin Just Bought TrainingPeaks — Here's What Changes for You — Garmin has acquired TrainingPeaks, and if your clients live and die by their training plans, this merger is worth watching. Platform integrations, pricing, and data ownership could all shift — so now's a good time to know where your programming lives. • New London gym puts recovery and wellness front and centre — London Post spotlights a brand-new gym built around injury recovery and wellness — a reminder for operators that the 'fix you up' niche is quietly becoming the whole pitch.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 22 Jul 2026
item.description: The $7.5B EGYM–Playlist deal, Planet Fitness walks back its strength pivot & Hyrox goes global • EGYM and Playlist's reported $7.5B tie-up could 'prescribe' fitness — The vision: structured training that works like a prescription — the right dose for the right person. Prescribed, measurable programming is becoming the product. • Planet Fitness admits its strength pivot went too far — A rare public walk-back. The lesson: read your own members before chasing a trend — overshooting a pivot costs more than moving deliberately. • Hyrox expands its global race calendar with multi-week events — Competitive fitness as mass participation keeps scaling worldwide. If your members love a challenge, Hyrox-style events are a proven engagement and revenue engine. • Les Mills launches its first-ever reformer Pilates program — The group-fitness giant is entering reformer Pilates. When Les Mills moves, thousands of studios follow — expect reformer programming to spread fast. • EoS Fitness opens 9 gyms in a single quarter — A blistering Texas-led expansion pace. Proof the value-gym model still has aggressive runway when the operations are dialed in. • Garmin debuts a $200 screenless band aimed at Whoop — A screenless tracker at $200, from a GPS-and-sensor heavyweight, lands right in Whoop's lane. The client conversation about subscription-free tracking just got easier.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 21 Jul 2026
item.description: Marc Lore's Wonder raises $650M, CorePower's CEO switch & ClassPass tilts toward studios • Marc Lore's Wonder raises $650M at a $9B valuation — Personalized, tech-driven nutrition just got a nine-figure bet. The signal for coaches: the food conversation is a premium service, and the personalization gap is where you win. • CorePower Yoga changes CEO, signals a growth push — New leadership at a major studio brand usually means new bets. Worth watching where CorePower places its chips — studio expansion, digital, or both. • ClassPass adds new studio-friendly policies and protections — When an aggregator works to keep studios happy, it needs your supply — and that's leverage. A good moment to revisit your own economics on the platform. • Engadget's review recap: the latest Oura Ring and more — A roundup of the season's notable gadgets, Oura included. Handy context next time a client asks whether the newest ring is actually worth the upgrade.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 20 Jul 2026
item.description: A longevity chain sells 200 territories, PureGym crosses to Ireland & the 'soft fitness' era • A longevity-center concept sells 200 franchise territories — Franchisees bought in before full launch — one of the strongest demand signals of the summer. The next franchise gold rush looks like longevity, not HIIT. • PureGym expands into Ireland, citing a growing market — The low-cost model still has runway in under-served geographies. A reminder that 'the market is saturated' is usually only true for one format at a time. • The 'soft fitness' era is officially here — Recovery, mobility, and low-intensity training are moving to the center of the offer. The stuff that used to be the cool-down is becoming the main event. • UFC Gym Murfreesboro opens with a recovery-first pitch — Even a combat-branded franchise is leading with full-service recovery. When the toughest names in fitness sell recovery up front, the trend has fully arrived.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 17 Jul 2026
item.description: Interactive Strength buys Stepr, NYC's click-to-cancel gym law & investors still love Pilates • Interactive Strength acquires connected stair-climber Stepr — Rolling specialized hardware under one roof, the deal signals the connected-fitness shakeout is entering its buy-or-be-bought phase. Watch who's next. • NYC becomes first US city to force click-to-cancel on gyms — The industry's oldest retention trick is on borrowed time. The winning move: make leaving easy and staying worth it, before regulation makes the choice for you. • Investors tip Pilates and low-price gyms for growth — Where the smart money sees the next few years. A useful map to check your own expansion or format bets against before you commit capital. • Echelon makes its case for connected strength — The connected-fitness brand is betting the next home category is strength, not cardio. Worth knowing where the equipment giants think the puck is going. • ATN Insights: driving revenue with movement assessments — A good assessment is better coaching and a natural upsell in one. If you're not screening new clients, you're leaving both results and revenue on the table.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 16 Jul 2026
item.description: Eli Lilly backs Oura for GLP-1 care, the Megaformer knockoff suit & why fitness AI isn't paying off • Eli Lilly takes a stake in Oura to support GLP-1 patients — A pharma giant betting on a smart ring says the wearable is where medication and lifestyle meet. Your GLP-1 clients need the human-plus-data support in between — build it. • Sebastien Lagree alleges a studio swapped Megaformers for knockoffs — The Pilates-equipment founder is fighting over counterfeits in Florida. If you run a licensed concept, it's a reminder that equipment IP is a real operational risk. • How Barry's turns employer wellness into a growth channel — The boutique giant is selling classes to companies, not just consumers. Corporate wellness is a B2B revenue line hiding in plain sight for studios with capacity to fill. • Why most fitness brands don't see a return on AI — New analysis says the AI spend isn't paying off yet. The fix: buy tools that remove a task, not tools that add another dashboard nobody checks. • ETS Performance tops 80 locations as youth fitness booms — A youth-performance chain crossing 80 sites confirms the kids' training market is real and scaling. A franchise lane worth studying if families are your audience. • YMCA vs the gyms: the best membership for your money — A consumer value comparison your members are quietly making too. Know how your pricing stacks up against the community-gym option before they ask.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 15 Jul 2026
item.description: Neko's $700M body-scan bet, Planet Fitness's lender drama & accountability beats motivation • Neko Health raises $700M at a $7B valuation — Daniel Ek's body-scanning startup is scaling preventive full-body scans ahead of a US launch. Serious capital is betting on 'catch it early' — a tailwind for outcome-led operators. • Lenders to a Planet Fitness operator eye taking ownership — A major franchisee's debt load is reportedly forcing a hard conversation. Proof that growth built on cheap leverage eventually sends a bill — watch your own. • NBA players sign a virtual-healthcare deal — Elite athletes putting their name to telehealth is a mainstreaming signal. Virtual health services are becoming a normal part of the performance stack, not a fringe add-on. • The Pilates craze comes for luxury real estate — Reformer studios are now an amenity developers advertise. When Pilates sells apartments, you're looking at a category with serious cultural staying power. • Accountability beats motivation for results, data shows — New data confirms what good coaches know: systems beat feelings. Motivation fades; accountability is a product you can actually sell and deliver. • Playlist launches an 'AI Concierge,' plans more AI — A studio-tech player leaning hard into AI-guided member experiences. Worth watching how automation shows up at the front desk, not just the back office.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 14 Jul 2026
item.description: Beckham's IM8 lands $1B, Whoop poaches a Nike CMO & DACH's fitness economy keeps growing • Beckham's IM8 secures $1B from General Catalyst — The supplement brand, owned by Prenetics, took a billion in growth capital via General Catalyst's Customer Value Fund. Wellness brands are now scaling like consumer giants. • Whoop names former Nike exec Dirk-Jan van Hameren as CMO — A recovery-tracker company hiring Nike-tier marketing talent tells you the next wearables battle is brand, not sensors. Storytelling is the new spec sheet. • Fitness brands are turning community into a marketing engine — The smartest operators are treating their member community as their growth channel. Cheaper than ads and impossible for a big-box competitor to copy overnight. • DACH fitness economy keeps growing, DSSV 2026 study finds — The German-speaking market is expanding, not plateauing. For operators in the region, the data says now is the time to scale with intention. • City Fitness crosses state lines with a Hoboken opening — The Philadelphia operator's first expansion outside its home city. A reminder that regional chains testing new markets is where a lot of 2026 growth is hiding.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 13 Jul 2026
item.description: Underwater headphones for swimmers, fitness's 'autonomous age' & the wearable-data problem • Zygo's underwater headphones are a game-changer for swimmers — Real-time audio and coaching cues in the pool solve a problem swimmers have always had. The coach on the deck can finally be in the athlete's ear. • How fitness brands can win in the 'autonomous age' — A wave of AI is set to reshape how wellness brands operate. The operators experimenting now will have a head start on the ones waiting for a playbook. • The wearable-data problem: more numbers, not better outcomes — The industry is quietly admitting that piling on tracker data hasn't made people healthier. The gap between data and behavior change is exactly where a human coach wins. • Athletech's 2026 State of the Industry: what's actually shaping fitness — A clear-eyed scan of the trends defining fitness and wellness this year. Worth ten minutes to make sure your 2026 plan is pointed at where the puck is going. • The most innovative fitness & wellness companies of 2026 — A useful map of who's setting the pace across the industry this year — and a shortlist of the players your members will start asking you about.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 10 Jul 2026
item.description: Youth fitness booms, members want 'sustainable' workouts & red-light recovery goes mainstream • Youth fitness is booming, despite the challenges — New data shows kids and teens entering structured fitness in bigger numbers. For operators, that's a real revenue line and a family-membership hook, not an afterthought. • Members want 'sustainable' workouts, report finds — Adults increasingly want training they can keep doing for decades, not a six-week crash. The shift from shred-for-summer to longevity is a messaging opportunity. • Kineon's Move+ stands out with a laser + red-light combo — A recovery device pairing two light therapies in one unit. Recovery tech keeps getting more accessible — worth knowing when clients ask what actually works. • ATN Insights: the power of the perfect playlist — The right music isn't decoration — it's a retention lever. A small, cheap detail most operators under-invest in, with an outsized effect on how a class feels. • The best July fitness & wellness deals worth flagging — From Lululemon to On Running, the summer sales are live. A handy shortlist when clients ask you what's actually worth buying right now.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 09 Jul 2026
item.description: Basic-Fit buys deeper into Germany, TRX goes retail & GLP-1s reshape the supplement aisle • Basic-Fit buys German brand wellyou for $61M — Europe's biggest low-cost chain is paying up to deepen its DACH footprint. For German-speaking operators, that's a giant getting bigger in your backyard — plan accordingly. • TRX launches a retail line, targeting the at-home market — The suspension-training brand is becoming its own retailer. A case study in owning the customer relationship instead of renting it through third parties. • How Vasa Fitness overhauled its digital strategy — A big-box operator rethinking its digital layer from the ground up. Useful reading for any studio deciding what to build, buy, or borrow online. • GLP-1s are quietly rewriting the supplement aisle — With demand for weight-loss drugs surging, supplement brands are racing to design products that complement them. The nutrition conversation with your clients just changed. • ATN Week in Review: the battle for competitive fitness — Competition formats are becoming a genuine business line, not a gimmick. If your members love a leaderboard, there's revenue in it — and the big chains know it.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 08 Jul 2026
item.description: A budget Whoop rival with ECG, Pilates hits 10 studios & Rhone bets on real-world retail • Loop Air: a Whoop rival with GPS and ECG at a budget price — Screenless, subscription-light, and it packs heart-rhythm and location data that used to be premium-only. The 'trackers are too expensive' objection is dissolving fast. • Vaura Pilates hits 10 studios with a Jakarta opening — Pilates isn't a Western trend anymore — it's a global format. Operators planting flags in new markets early are the ones building brand equity now. • Rhone's CEO doubles down on in-person shopping and events — In a world drowning in performance ads, betting on real-world community as a sales channel is contrarian — and worth watching for any community-led operator. • Garmin's Forerunner 70 lands in India — Another entry-level tracker shipping into a high-volume market. The action in wearables right now is at the affordable end — where most of your clients actually live. • Therabody's post-Prime-Day sale keeps running — Recovery gear is still discounted well past the event. A timely window if you're kitting out a studio recovery corner or fielding client buying questions.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 07 Jul 2026
item.description: Life Time bets on competition, Pilates keeps scaling & Hand & Stone rethinks the upsell • Life Time turns members into competitors in Dallas — LT Games expands as the fitness-competition race heats up. Turning members into athletes with a date on the calendar is retention dressed up as an event. • JetSet Pilates keeps expanding, sets new growth targets — The reformer brand isn't slowing its unit count. If Pilates is on your radar, the franchise math is being written in real time — worth studying before you commit. • Hand & Stone makes relaxation the headline, not the add-on — By selling calm as the destination rather than an upsell, Hand & Stone can price it directly. A framing lesson for any operator burying recovery in a membership. • Fitness On Demand beefs up its boutique content library — A bigger on-demand catalog aimed at boutique studios — useful ammo for operators who want a digital layer without building one from scratch. • Fitness World Canada plans to open gyms in the US — A rare south-bound expansion. When an established Canadian operator eyes the US market, it's a signal about where they think the growth runway still is. • This season's best fitness & wellness launches, from Oura and more — A roundup of the summer's notable product drops. Handy shortlist for coaches fielding the inevitable 'what should I actually buy?' from clients.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 06 Jul 2026
item.description: Strength training's longevity sweet spot, CrossFit builds a wearable & women's strength goes boutique • The strength-training 'sweet spot' for a longer life — New research pins down how much lifting actually moves the longevity needle — and it's less than you'd think. For coaches, it reframes strength work as healthspan work. • CrossFit taps WurQ to build a functional-fitness wearable — CrossFit is co-developing a tracker built around how functional athletes actually move. If you coach functional training, your clients are about to have a data layer built for them. • Founders Row bets the boutique format on women's strength — New concept LiftHer puts lifting — not spin or barre — at the center of a women's studio brand. A clear read on where boutique momentum is heading. • Pure Barre's studio-wide livestream draws a big crowd — A single synchronized livestream across the whole franchise pulled a big turnout — a reminder that digital events can drive in-studio community, not cannibalize it. • Contrast hydrotherapy goes mainstream in recovery — Hot-cold contrast protocols are moving from elite sport into everyday recovery menus. An easy, low-capex add-on for studios chasing the recovery dollar. • Lars Springer crowned European champion — A DACH win worth a nod — proof the region's strength scene is producing podium talent, and a marketing hook for German-speaking studios that coach the discipline.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 03 Jul 2026
item.description: The IRL Comeback: Bodies, Boutiques, and Brick-and-Mortar • Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why — The Global Wellness Institute is putting contrast hydrotherapy on the radar, and for coaches and studio operators, that means one thing: your clients are about to start asking questions. Get ahead of it. • Rhone Is Betting Big on Brick-and-Mortar and IRL Events — The activewear brand's CEO says they're doubling down on in-person shopping and events — a reminder that for fitness-adjacent brands, the gym floor (and the store floor) still beats the scroll. • Fitness On Demand Just Got a Boutique Upgrade — Fitness On Demand has partnered with JETSWEAT to bring more boutique studio content to its platform — meaning operators can now offer members that curated, high-energy class feel without needing to actually build out the studio. • Rs 1.8 Crore Bet: Heatronics Is Heating Up the Wellness Device Space — Indian startup Heatronics just closed a Rs 1.8 crore raise to scale its wellness device business. If you're watching the hardware side of recovery and performance tech, this one's worth keeping on your radar. • Heatronics Heats Up With Rs 1.8 Cr Seed Round — Wellness device startup Heatronics has raised Rs 1.8 crore in seed funding led by Inflection Point Ventures. The fresh capital will fuel expansion of its wellness device business. • Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld Has the Receipts — Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case for exercise as a genuine therapeutic tool — a timely reminder for coaches and studio owners that the science behind what you do is your strongest sales pitch.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 02 Jul 2026
item.description: The 90-Minute Strength Rule Changes How You Program • Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why — The Global Wellness Institute is shining a spotlight on contrast hydrotherapy as a rising recovery modality. If you're not already talking hot-cold protocols with your clients, now's a good time to warm up to the idea. • Founders Row launches LiftHer, a women's strength studio — Founders Row is entering the women's strength space with its new LiftHer studio concept — a signal that dedicated female lifting environments are moving from niche to next big thing for operators watching where boutique dollars are flowing. • The Best Fitness & Wellness Launches This Season, Ranked — From the Oura Ring 5 to an AI-powered scented sleep gadget, Athletech News rounded up this season's most notable fitness and wellness drops — worth a scan if you're advising clients on gear or scouting what's actually cutting through the noise. • The Weekly Workout Sweet Spot for a Longer Life? 90–119 Mins — A new study covered by the Global Wellness Institute found that 90–119 minutes of strength training per week may be the magic window for longevity. Not too little, not too much — just right, says the science.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 01 Jul 2026
item.description: Your Clients Want Less Lifting and More Cold Plunges • Contrast Hydrotherapy Is Having a Moment — Here's Why — The Global Wellness Institute is shining a spotlight on contrast hydrotherapy as a rising recovery modality. If you're programming recovery for clients, this is the trend worth getting ahead of before your competitors do. • Pure Barre Pulls Big Numbers With Studio-Wide Livestream — Pure Barre's studio-wide livestream drew serious turnout — proof that when you give members a reason to show up (even virtually), they will. For operators eyeing community-at-scale plays, this one's worth a look. • CrossFit and WurQ Are Building a Functional Fitness Wearable — CrossFit has partnered with WurQ to develop a wearable built specifically for functional fitness movements — which means the data your clients actually care about might finally have a device to match.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 30 Jun 2026
item.description: Your Biology Is Outvoting You • Your Brain on a Bad Day: The Biology Behind Tough Calls — The Global Wellness Institute unpacks how pressure physically reshapes the way leaders make decisions — which means if you're running a studio on four hours of sleep and a cold brew, your biology is quietly voting against you. • Boutique Studios: Your Old Marketing Playbook Is Expiring Fast — Athletech News flags that marketing tactics for boutique fitness studios are shifting at serious speed. If your strategy still looks like 2022, it might be time to tear up the script and write a new one. • GLP-1 drugs work better with exercise, study confirms — A new multinational study finds that exercise improves the long-term benefits of GLP-1 medications — which means your clients on Ozempic aren't off the hook, they're actually your best argument for showing up consistently. • Garmin Forerunner 170 Music lands a Women's Running verdict — Women's Running just dropped its review of the Garmin Forerunner 170 Music — if you're recommending GPS watches to clients who want beats with their splits, this one's worth a read before your next gear conversation. • Oura Ring 5 Is a 'Generational Leap' for Smart Rings — The Guardian calls the Oura Ring 5 a stunning generational upgrade — for coaches and studio owners recommending wearables to clients, it just got a lot easier to point to a clear leader in the category. • Movement is Therapy — and Wolfgang Unsöld has the receipts — Trainingsworld spotlights Wolfgang Unsöld's case that movement isn't just training — it's treatment. For coaches and studio operators, that's a positioning goldmine: your floor isn't a gym, it's a clinic with better playlists.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 29 Jun 2026
item.description: Scaling Before Ready: The New Boutique Playbook • SotoMethod Takes Its NYC Sweat Formula to Dallas — The boutique studio is making its first move outside New York City, planting a flag in Dallas as the opening act of a national expansion. For studio operators watching the boutique space, it's a reminder that the brands scaling right now aren't waiting for perfect conditions — they're just picking a city and going. • Your Brain on Pressure: The Biology of Leading Under Stress — The Global Wellness Institute is diving into the biology of decision-making under pressure — because it turns out your leadership style and your nervous system are in a complicated relationship. • The Global Strength Equipment Market Just Got a Full Breakdown — IndexBox dropped a deep-dive analysis on the world strength training equipment market — size, trends, forecasts, the works. If you're planning your next equipment investment or expanding your floor, this is the kind of data that turns gut feelings into actual strategy. • Garmin Forerunner 170 Music reviewed by Women's Running — Women's Running put Garmin's Forerunner 170 Music through its paces — if you're recommending GPS watches to running clients, this hands-on take is worth a look before your next gear conversation. • Brownlee & Bryant Are Bringing AI to the Fitness Congress — Triathlon legend Alistair Brownlee and performance expert Steffie Bryant are headlining the Health & Fitness Association's European Congress with a session on AI and performance insights — if you're a coach or operator wondering how to actually use AI beyond the hype, this one's worth your calendar. • Wareable just ranked the best fitness trackers of 2026 — Wareable dropped their fully reviewed, tested, and compared guide to the top fitness trackers on the market right now. If clients keep asking what to wear on their wrist, you finally have a cheat sheet worth bookmarking.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 26 Jun 2026
item.description: Identity is infrastructure — for bodies, brands, and cities • Your Brain on Leadership: Why Pressure Rewires Your Calls — The Global Wellness Institute is digging into the biology behind high-stakes decision-making — and for coaches and studio owners, understanding what stress actually does to your brain mid-crunch could be the difference between a smart pivot and a panic move. • Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Becoming Wellness Gold — Lokmattimes spotlights how sustainability and authentic Thai hospitality are shaping the wellness scene in Krabi and Koh Lanta — worth a look if you're scouting retreat destinations or want to know where your clients are dragging their yoga mats next. • SotoMethod Takes Its NYC Sweat Formula to Dallas — The boutique studio is planting its first flag outside New York City, kicking off a national expansion push. For operators watching the boutique space, it's a reminder that a strong method plus the right moment can turn a single location into a growth story. • Cannes 2026 Adds 6 New Categories—Here's What's In — Audio & Radio, Health & Wellness, Pharma, Creative B2B, Print & Publishing, and Outdoor are all getting their own spotlight at Cannes Lions 2026 — meaning the work your studio does to market itself just got a much bigger stage to compete on. • 21-Year-Old PT on How Top Surgery Reshaped His Fitness Identity — People.com spotlights a young personal trainer whose top surgery and fitness journey became inseparable parts of finding himself. For coaches, it's a reminder that identity isn't a sidebar to training — sometimes it's the whole program. • Sport Alliance is rewriting fitness's AI playbook — body LIFE reports that Sport Alliance is actively reshaping how the fitness industry approaches AI — meaning the tools your competitors are experimenting with today could be table stakes by tomorrow.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 25 Jun 2026
item.description: The fitness industry is getting a biology upgrade • Your Brain on a Bad Day: The Biology Behind Big Calls — The Global Wellness Institute is digging into how pressure physically hijacks your decision-making — which means the way you show up to tough conversations with staff or clients isn't just mindset, it's biology. • Crunch Fitness Has a New Boss: Meet Chequan Lewis — Crunch Fitness has named Chequan Lewis as its new CEO in what the company is calling a major leadership move. For operators and studio founders, it's worth watching — leadership changes at a chain this size tend to signal where the big-box market is headed next. • Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI — Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively transforming how the fitness sector operates through AI — meaning the tech your competitors are quietly testing today could be the standard your studio is judged against tomorrow. • Swiss Brand Loya Is Coming for Your Grey Hairs — Longevity label Loya has launched an anti-grey haircare line, bringing the wellness-meets-science positioning that sells so well in fitness into the beauty aisle. If your clients are already buying into recovery tech and biohacking, this is the next category they'll be spending on. • Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Rewriting the Wellness Retreat Playbook — Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into something coaches and retreat operators can't easily replicate at home. If you're scouting locations for an immersive program, this corner of the Gulf is worth more than a casual Google. • Health & Fitness Association Is Taking Its Show to Asia — The Health & Fitness Association is expanding its presence across Asia through presentations at major industry events. For operators eyeing growth beyond their home markets, consider this your cue to pay attention.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 24 Jun 2026
item.description: The infrastructure of legitimacy is being built around you • Lifestyle Medicine Is Now a Cancer Care Power Player — The Global Wellness Institute says lifestyle interventions matter across the entire cancer journey — from prevention all the way through survivorship. For coaches and studio owners, that's a signal: your work isn't just wellness fluff, it's clinically relevant territory worth leaning into. • Crunch Fitness Has a New CEO — Meet Chequan Lewis — Crunch Fitness has tapped Chequan Lewis as its new chief executive in what the chain is calling a major leadership move. For operators watching the big-box space, a fresh face at the top usually signals a strategic reset — worth keeping an eye on where Crunch points next. • Cycling BC Keeps the Recovery Rolling With 360 Athletics — Cycling BC has renewed its partnership with Recovery Room, the sister brand of 360 Athletics — a reminder that for sports orgs and operators alike, the recovery offering is becoming just as important as the ride itself. • Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Becoming Wellness Operator Gold — Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into a compelling wellness travel package. For retreat operators and studio founders eyeing international programming, this region is worth a serious look before everyone else catches on. • Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI — Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively changing how the fitness sector works through artificial intelligence — meaning the tech wave isn't coming for coaches and operators, it's already in the building. • A NZ supplement brand just pulled creatine — now what? — A New Zealand supplement brand has yanked creatine from shelves, leaving a gap in a lot of client stacks. Worth knowing your alternatives before someone asks you mid-session.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 21 Jun 2026
item.description: Hardware gets smaller; the fitness stakes get bigger • Peloton snaps up connected Pilates brand Skōp — Peloton has acquired Skōp, a connected Pilates innovator, as it looks to broaden its fitness portfolio beyond the bike. For studio operators eyeing the connected-equipment space, this signals Pilates is officially too big for anyone to ignore. • Rajnish Wellness: Big Revenue, Bigger Losses in Q4 FY26 — The wellness brand posted a revenue surge last quarter — but heavy losses mean growth alone isn't paying the bills. For studio operators, it's a timely reminder that top-line momentum means nothing if the bottom line is bleeding out. • Oura Ring 5 Is 40% Smaller and Now Tracks Blood Pressure — The latest Oura Ring just got a serious hardware upgrade — smaller form factor, plus blood pressure tracking that could make it a much easier sell to clients who hate wearing 'gear.' For coaches and studio operators, wearable buy-in just got a lower barrier. • This AI Pin Records Everything You Say—Hands-Free — The Plaud NotePin S is a wearable AI recorder that captures conversations without you touching your phone. For coaches drowning in session notes and client check-ins, that's a pretty compelling reason to pin something new to your kit bag. • Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show — From day one to today, Fitbod's CEO says resistance training has never lost its throne — and it's the same logic fueling the rise of AI coaching tools like Google's Health Coach. For operators, the message is clear: build your offering around the iron, and the tech will follow.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 16 Jun 2026
item.description: Investors double down on fitness • Investors double down on fitness — Capital is rotating back into the category after a quiet stretch. Funded competitors mean faster-moving markets — and richer exit options for operators. • Recovery funding surges — Money is chasing rest as hard as it once chased reps. Recovery is becoming its own profit center, not a wellness afterthought. • AI coaching scales — Automated programming and check-ins are getting good enough to handle the bottom of your client ladder. Use it to free your time for the high-touch tier that actually pays.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 12 Jun 2026
item.description: Studio M&A heats up • Studio M&A heats up — Consolidation is accelerating as larger groups roll up independents. Know your numbers — you're either an acquirer, a target, or roadkill. • Wearables add UV tracking — Sensors keep expanding into everyday health metrics, deepening the data clients bring you. The coach's edge is interpretation, not collection. • Menopause fitness goes mainstream — A once-taboo market is getting purpose-built programming and real budgets. Specializing here is one of the clearest white-space plays in fitness right now.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 11 Jun 2026
item.description: A big-box gym CEO heads for the exit • A big-box CEO exits — Leadership churn at a fitness giant is a tell that the mass-membership model is under strain. Boutique operators should read it as an opening, not just gossip. • A new hydration brand launches — Another entrant in the electrolyte gold rush shows how cheap a beverage is to launch — and how crowded the shelf has become. Differentiation now lives in distribution, not formula. • Alcohol's risks resurface — Fresh health warnings keep nudging clients toward sober-curious habits. Coaches who frame training as the better high have the cultural wind at their back.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 10 Jun 2026
item.description: Creatine eats the supplement aisle • Supplements add creatine — Creatine is escaping the bro-science aisle into mainstream wellness SKUs. An easy, evidence-backed upsell for coaches who'd rather sell trust than hype. • Recovery tech expands — Saunas, cold plunges and compression are becoming standalone revenue lines, not perks. Increasingly the recovery room can out-earn the weight room. • Creator brands scale up — Fitness creators are turning audiences into product lines at speed. The lesson for operators: your following is inventory you haven't monetized yet.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 09 Jun 2026
item.description: Pilates keeps winning • Pilates keeps winning — Low-impact, high-margin and endlessly shareable, Pilates keeps stealing share from traditional gyms. If you don't offer a version of it, your churned members already found one. • Athletes reject soda — Elite athletes ditching sugary drinks is handing functional-beverage brands their marketing script. Expect the trickle-down to reshape what sells at your front desk. • Women's health raises $215M — Capital is finally flowing into a long-ignored market. The studios and apps that design for women specifically — not 'shrink it and pink it' — will own the upside.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 05 Jun 2026
item.description: Longevity goes commercial • Longevity goes commercial — What was a biohacker niche is turning into shelves, clinics and memberships. Early movers are productizing 'live longer' before the mainstream catches on. • Non-alc doubles down — Zero-proof is no longer a January gimmick — brands are building year-round businesses. For studios, sober social events are a cheap, on-brand retention play. • Food-as-medicine breaks through — Insurers and employers are starting to pay for nutrition as treatment. Coaches who can speak the clinical language plug into budgets that dwarf consumer wallets.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — 04 Jun 2026
item.description: Longevity's $435M vote of confidence • A $435M longevity raise — The biggest longevity bet yet says real money is chasing healthspan, not just lifespan. Coaches who sell 'add good years to your life' now have a funded category at their back. • Immersive fitness returns — Screen-led classes and VR workouts are back as operators hunt for experiences clients can't replicate at home. The differentiator is no longer the equipment — it's the room. • Personalized health expands — From bloodwork to wearables, 'built for you' is becoming table stakes. Generic, one-size programs are getting harder to charge premium prices for.
